§1 — Week in Review

The Week That Confirmed Everything — Defensive Rotation Has Arrived

This was the week the NYSE McClellan Oscillator divergence finally resolved. SOXX fell −11.24% on Friday alone. Defensive sectors held. The UVCMI 777™ 20/20 Vision™ screen called it before price confirmed. Here is the full SS36 close.

Key Observation: XSD and SOXX — the two semiconductor ETFs — are the biggest losers on the day despite having the highest YTD B1 readings (+7,667 and +7,765). This is classic distribution of leadership: the strongest performers of the year are being sold hardest. High YTD gains + sudden sharp decline = rotation out of leaders, not a dip to buy.

§3 — ST Screen · 1H / 2H / 4H

Short-Term UVCMI — Selling Pressure Across All Frames

Dr. UV's SS36 · ST Screen · UVCMI 1H/2H/4H · SOXX 1H: −723 · QQQ 1H: −442 · XSD 1H: −743 · Orange crossing lines mark momentum reversal · June 5, 2026

📊 ST Screen Read

Defensive sectors holding ST: XLP, IDU, XLRE, XLU all showing positive or near-zero 1H/2H/4H readings — intraday buyers active in defensives. This is real money rotating, not just algorithmic noise.

Tech/Semi devastation ST: SOXX 1H: −723 · XSD 1H: −743 · QQQ 1H: −442 · XLK 1H: −556 — all deeply negative across every short-term frame. No intraday recovery attempts holding. This is sustained selling, not a flash dip.

The orange crossing lines: Sectors that were positive through the week are crossing into negative territory simultaneously — the visual signature of a sector-wide regime change. When 20+ sectors cross from green to red in the same session, breadth is deteriorating rapidly.

DIA (Dow) 1H: −171 — even the defensive-tilted Dow is getting hit, but significantly less than QQQ/SOXX. The Dow's relative outperformance confirms defensive rotation within equities.

§4 — IT-LT Screen · D / 2D / 3D / W

Intermediate-Long Term — Weekly UVCMI Being Consumed

Dr. UV's SS36 · IT-LT Screen · UVCMI D/2D/3D/W · XSD W: massive positive being consumed · XLP/XLU W still holding · SPY highlighted blue · June 5, 2026

📊 IT-LT Screen Read

The weekly consumption pattern: XSD and SOXX have enormous positive weekly UVCMI readings accumulated over months of leadership — but the daily readings are now deeply negative and consuming those weekly gains rapidly. This is distribution: smart money selling into accumulated weekly strength.

Defensive weekly structure intact: XLP W: +229 · XLU W positive · XLRE W: +437 · IDU W: +290 — these defensive sectors have maintained positive weekly UVCMI even through today's broad selling. This means the defensive rotation has intermediate-term legs, not just a one-day flight to safety.

SPY (highlighted blue row): SPY D: −92 · W: +681 — the daily is negative but the weekly is still positive. This tells you the intermediate trend has not yet broken — this is a correction within a bull market, not a full trend reversal. Watch for SPY W to turn negative as the confirmation signal that would change the assessment.

XLY (Consumer Discretionary) W: deteriorating — consumer spending confidence weakening alongside the tech selloff. When discretionary joins the laggards, it signals economic concern, not just sector rotation.

§5 — UVCMI 20/20 Vision™ · SS36

"Last 10 KMs" — The Final Leg of the Warning

The SS36 UVCMI 20/20 Vision™ screen — 20 simultaneous timeframes across all 36 sectors. Dr. UV annotated this screen "LAST 10 KMS" — meaning the final leg of the warning arc is now in progress.

UVCMI 20/20 Vision™ — SS36

20 TIMEFRAMES · 36 SECTORS · PROPRIETARY DR. UV VIEW

The "Last 10 KMs" annotation refers to a marathon analogy — the final stretch where everything hurts most but the finish line is near. In market terms: the divergence has been building for weeks (miles 1-32), the MO crossed below zero (mile 33), price broke support today (mile 36). We are in the final resolution phase.

The orange crossing patterns across the full 20-timeframe view show multiple X formations — each X marks a sector transitioning from bull to bear across multiple simultaneous timeframes. When you see this many X patterns in a single session, the breadth deterioration is systemic, not isolated.

Dr. UV's SS36 · UVCMI 20/20 Vision™ · 20 Simultaneous Timeframes · "LAST 10 KMS" annotation · Multiple orange X crossing patterns · Full sector regime change visible · June 5, 2026
§6 — Full UVCMI 777™ · All 36 Sectors

Complete Multi-Timeframe View — The Orange X Pattern

Dr. UV's SS36 · UVCMI 777™ All Timeframes · Orange X patterns mark simultaneous sector reversals · SPY highlighted blue (center) · Full regime change confirmed · June 5, 2026

📊 Full UVCMI 777™ Read — What the X Pattern Means

Multiple simultaneous X formations: Each orange X in this screen marks a sector where positive UVCMI momentum is crossing into negative simultaneously across multiple timeframes. Four or more X formations in the same session is rare — it signals a systemic, cross-sector momentum shift, not sector-specific rebalancing.

The blue highlighted row (SPY): SPY sits at the inflection point — still positive on longer timeframes but negative on short and intermediate. This is the critical zone: if SPY's weekly UVCMI follows the daily negative, the correction deepens. If SPY weekly holds, this is a correction within an intact bull trend.

What comes next per UVCMI 777™: Watch for NYSE MO to recover above zero (currently −18.61). Watch for SOXX to stabilize — as the Lead Scout, when SOXX finds a UVCMI floor and begins rebuilding green clusters, it will signal the earliest re-entry opportunity. Until then, defensive sectors (XLP, XLU, XLRE, XLV) are the mathematical safe harbor.

DR. UV's SS36 Tracker™ · B-Screen™ · RelS vs SPY™ · DR UVC Universal™
UVCMI 777™ is Dr. UV's Master Indicator calculated from a single proprietary formula incorporating the last 60 units of closing prices. Runs across 7 simultaneous timeframes (1H, 2H, 4H, D, 2D, 3D, W). UVCMI 20/20 Vision™ extends this to 20 simultaneous timeframes for broad diagnostic purposes. Green = positive momentum · Red = negative momentum.
